description: Add an inline embedding-similarity guard to the loop-engine learning auto-extraction path so cross-iteration semantic near-duplicates are skipped at write time instead of polluting recall until the post-hoc curate dedup pass.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests pass with `cargo test` for the touched module
No breaking changes to existing public APIs (ExtractionResult shape, learning_exists signature) unless explicitly required
No literal Claude model strings introduced outside model.rs (tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Bias toward false negatives, not false positives — a wrongly-dropped distinct learning is unrecoverable; a missed dupe is cleaned up by curate dedup later
Graceful degradation — Ollama down must be byte-identical to today's exact-match-only behavior
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that assert try_embed_* returns 0 or 'Ollama is down' — they fail when local Ollama is actually running (learning #1513). Test the PURE cosine decision (find_near_duplicate) with synthetic vectors instead.
Silently dropping a learning when the embedding call errors mid-batch — uncertainty must record, never skip.
Bypassing the LearningWriter chokepoint by calling store_embedding inline to avoid the flush re-embed.
Applying the new guard to the human `task-mgr learn` path or import_learnings — ingestion auto-extraction ONLY.
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check a type without verifying the similarity/skip behavior.

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: best_match returns Some((id, sim)) for the single highest-similarity known entry regardless of threshold
Positive: find_near_duplicate == best_match filtered to sim >= threshold
Positive: NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD == 0.92 (f32) with a doc comment stating the asymmetric-risk rationale; NEAR_MISS_LOG_FLOOR == 0.80
Negative: best_match / find_near_duplicate return None on an empty `known` slice
Negative: find_near_duplicate returns None when all cosine similarities are strictly below threshold
Negative: NearDuplicateChecker has NO `model` struct field — cargo clippy/check pass with -D warnings (no dead_code on an unread field)
Unit test: two identical vectors -> cosine 1.0 -> best_match returns Some with that id
Unit test: orthogonal vectors (cosine 0) with threshold 0.92 -> find_near_duplicate None
Unit test: boundary — a pair whose cosine == threshold is treated as a duplicate by find_near_duplicate (>= not >)
Unit test: among three known vectors with sims 0.93/0.97/0.95, best_match returns the 0.97 id (picks max, not first)
Known-bad: an implementation that returns the FIRST match (not the max) fails the 0.93/0.97/0.95 ordering test — the test must assert the max-similarity id, not just 'a match'
Known-bad: using `> threshold` instead of `>= threshold` in find_near_duplicate fails the exactly-at-threshold boundary test
Failure mode: NearDuplicateChecker::new returns None (not Err, no panic) when Ollama is unreachable — verifiable by pointing read_project_config's ollama_url at an unreachable host via a temp config
Failure mode: NearDuplicateChecker::new returns None (logs a warning) if load_all_active_embeddings errors — exact-match-only is preserved

Observability: check() emits a near-miss line ('near-miss #<id> cos=<sim:.3> (< <threshold>)') when NEAR_MISS_LOG_FLOOR <= best sim < threshold, then returns Unique — verifiable via best_match purely: a 0.85-similar vector is the best_match max yet find_near_duplicate(.., 0.92) is None, so the Unique arm (not Duplicate) must be taken. Do NOT implement check() with only the >= threshold arm and a bare Unique else — that skips the near-miss log the best_match/find_near_duplicate split exists to enable
Documentation: a code comment at the new()/load path states cross-model comparison is intentionally skipped (only configured-model embeddings are loaded)

CONTRACT: load_all_active_embeddings returns Vec<LearningEmbedding> (struct with .learning_id and .embedding) — adapt to (i64, Vec<f32>) via .map; grep the struct def in embeddings/mod.rs to confirm field names
userStories.description:
Add the reusable near-duplicate machinery to src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs. Two consts, two pure fns, an enum, and a checker — all decision logic unit-tested without Ollama.

DO:
- Add `pub const NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD: f32 = 0.92;` with a doc comment explaining the asymmetric-risk rationale (false positive silently drops a real learning with no LLM second opinion; bias high, well above curate's 0.65 pre-cluster value). Add `const NEAR_MISS_LOG_FLOOR: f32 = 0.80;` for the near-miss calibration band.
- Add pure fn `best_match(candidate: &[f32], known: &[(i64, Vec<f32>)]) -> Option<(i64, f32)>` returning the single highest-similarity known entry (by cosine_similarity) REGARDLESS of threshold, else None on empty `known`. Reuse the existing `cosine_similarity` fn. No I/O. This is the testable primitive (review finding #3).
- Add pure fn `find_near_duplicate(candidate, known, threshold) -> Option<(i64, f32)>` = `best_match(candidate, known).filter(|(_, s)| *s >= threshold)`. No I/O.
- Add `pub enum NearDupOutcome { Duplicate { existing_id: i64, similarity: f32 }, Unique(Vec<f32>), Unavailable }`.
- Add `pub struct NearDuplicateChecker { embedder: OllamaEmbedder, threshold: f32, known: Vec<(i64, Vec<f32>)> }`. NO owned `model` field — the OllamaEmbedder already retains its model internally; a second unread field trips `dead_code` under -D warnings (review finding #2).
  - `pub fn new(conn: &Connection, db_dir: &Path, threshold: f32) -> Option<Self>`: resolve ollama_url + embedding_model ONCE into LOCAL bindings via read_project_config (mirror try_embed_learning at embeddings/mod.rs ~375); build OllamaEmbedder::new(&url, &model); return None unless is_available() is Ok(true); then call load_all_active_embeddings(conn, &model) — on Ok map each LearningEmbedding via `.map(|le| (le.learning_id, le.embedding))` into `known`; on Err, eprintln a warning and return None (review finding #4 — exact-match-only, not an empty-known checker). The local `model` is used for BOTH the embedder and the loader, then dropped.
  - `pub fn check(&self, title: &str, content: &str) -> NearDupOutcome`: compose_embed_text(title, content); if empty -> Unavailable; embed once via self.embedder; on Err -> Unavailable (fire-and-forget, never propagate); else call best_match(&emb, &self.known): Some((id,sim)) with sim>=threshold -> Duplicate{existing_id:id, similarity:sim}; Some((id,sim)) with NEAR_MISS_LOG_FLOOR<=sim<threshold -> eprintln a near-miss line (`near-miss #<id> cos=<sim:.3> (< <threshold>)`) then Unique(emb); otherwise Unique(emb).
  - `pub fn register(&mut self, id: i64, embedding: Vec<f32>)`: push (id, embedding) onto self.known so a later same-batch candidate is compared against it.

DO NOT:
- Do NOT store the model as a struct field (review finding #2) — resolve once locally in new().
- Do NOT call read_project_config more than once per checker.
- Do NOT write tests that assume Ollama is down (learning #1513) — test best_match / find_near_duplicate purely.
- Do NOT compare across embedding models — only rows stored under the configured model are loaded; intentional, document in a code comment.

Edge cases:
- Empty known set -> best_match / find_near_duplicate return None.
- Exactly-at-threshold cosine -> counts as a match (>=).
- Multiple matches -> best_match returns the single highest-similarity one.
- Empty composed text -> check() returns Unavailable.
- load_all_active_embeddings Err -> new() returns None.
edgeCases: Cosine exactly at threshold: treated as duplicate (>=)
Multiple above-threshold matches: return the maximum, not the first
Empty composed embed text: check() -> Unavailable
Ollama unreachable at construction: new() -> None

failureModes.cause: Ollama unreachable / model missing at new()
failureModes.expectedBehavior: new() returns None; caller degrades to exact-match guard
new() logs a warning and returns None; exact-match-only preserved (review finding #4)

failureModes.cause: embed() errors for a single candidate mid-batch
failureModes.expectedBehavior: check() returns Unavailable; caller records the candidate (never drops on uncertainty)

userStories.notes: Reuse: cosine_similarity, compose_embed_text, OllamaEmbedder (new/embed/is_available/model), load_all_active_embeddings, LearningEmbedding — all in src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs. read_project_config from src/loop_engine/project_config.rs (ollama_url: Option<String>, embedding_model: Option<String>); DEFAULT_OLLAMA_URL / DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL consts already exist in embeddings/mod.rs — mirror try_embed_learning's resolution exactly, but bind model to a LOCAL (not a struct field — review finding #2). best_match is the pure primitive; find_near_duplicate filters it; check() reuses best_match for both the decision and the near-miss log (review finding #3). load error -> warn + None (review finding #4). Learning #1414: embedding pre-filter via cosine on stored embeddings is the established dedup pattern. Learning #1658: vector backend already loads via load_all_active_embeddings and post-filters in Rust — same loader. Learning #1513: do NOT write Ollama-down-assuming tests. Learning #2670: degrade gracefully for optional services (return None).
qualityDimensions: best_match / find_near_duplicate are pure (no I/O) and deterministic — unit-testable without Ollama
Single max-similarity match returned; find_near_duplicate uses >= threshold semantics
Embedding model resolved exactly once (LOCAL binding in new(), no owned struct field) — no dead_code warning, no config-drift no-op
Graceful degradation: new() -> None on Ollama-unavailable AND on load error; check() -> Unavailable on any Ollama failure; never panic/propagate
Public items documented; cross-model-skip is an explicit code comment
userStories.title: Add embedding near-duplicate primitives (const, pure fn, checker)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: record_extracted_learnings is a private helper taking Option<&mut dyn NearDupGuard>; extract_learnings_from_output delegates to it after spawn/parse/enrich and flushes after
Positive: a scripted NearDupGuard fake returning Duplicate{..} causes the candidate to NOT be recorded and deduped to increment
Positive: a fake returning Unique(emb) records the candidate AND register() is called with (new id, emb)
Positive: a fake returning Unavailable records the candidate (never dropped)
Positive: a Duplicate skip logs the existing learning id and the cosine similarity
Negative: when guard is None, behavior is byte-identical to before — only Tier-1 exact-match dedup runs
Negative: ExtractionResult shape and learning_exists signature are unchanged
Test (no Ollama): record_extracted_learnings with guard=None, two params with distinct titles -> both record
Test (no Ollama): record_extracted_learnings with guard=None, two params with identical (outcome,title) -> second skipped by Tier-1 (Tier-1-runs-first regression guard)
Test (no Ollama): record_extracted_learnings with a fake guard scripted Duplicate then Unique -> first deduped, second recorded+registered (exercises all arms without Ollama)

Known-bad: an implementation that runs Tier-2 BEFORE Tier-1, or skips Tier-1 when a guard exists, breaks the guard=None exact-match test and the 'Tier-1 unconditional' guarantee — the test must confirm exact-match fires with no guard
Known-bad: forgetting to call guard.register() on a Unique record lets same-batch dupes through — the scripted-fake test asserts register was invoked with the recorded id
Failure mode: if NearDuplicateChecker::new returns None, extract_learnings_from_output passes guard=None and records all non-exact-dup learnings
CONTRACT: params.outcome + params.title feed learning_exists; params.title + params.content feed check(); grep RecordLearningParams in src/learnings/crud to confirm field names
userStories.description:
Add tier-2 semantic dedup to the recording loop in extract_learnings_from_output (src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs), via a TEST SEAM so the Tier-2 arms are unit-testable without a subprocess or Ollama.

This modifies the side effects of extract_learnings_from_output: when Ollama is up, it skips inserting extracted learnings whose embedding is >= NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD cosine-similar to an existing active learning or an earlier-accepted candidate in the same batch. Callers (loop_engine iteration pipeline, learning #2135) consume only ExtractionResult{learnings_extracted, learning_ids}; the shape is unchanged — they simply observe fewer rows. The existing `deduped` counter / 'duplicate(s) skipped' log already exists; extend it.

TEST SEAM (review finding #1) — extract_learnings_from_output calls spawn_claude (~mod.rs:105) BEFORE the recording loop, so the loop is unreachable from a unit test without a subprocess. Extract the loop:
- Add a PRIVATE trait `NearDupGuard { fn check(&self, title: &str, content: &str) -> NearDupOutcome; fn register(&mut self, id: i64, embedding: Vec<f32>); }` and `impl NearDupGuard for NearDuplicateChecker` (forwards to the inherent methods from FEAT-001).
- Add a PRIVATE helper `fn record_extracted_learnings(conn: &Connection, writer: &mut LearningWriter, params_list: Vec<RecordLearningParams>, guard: Option<&mut dyn NearDupGuard>) -> TaskMgrResult<(Vec<i64>, usize)>` returning (learning_ids, deduped). All Tier-1/Tier-2 logic moves here.
- extract_learnings_from_output becomes: spawn -> parse -> enrich -> `let mut checker = db_dir.and_then(|d| NearDuplicateChecker::new(conn, d, NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD));` -> `record_extracted_learnings(conn, &mut writer, params_list, checker.as_mut().map(|c| c as &mut dyn NearDupGuard))` -> writer.flush(conn).
This is DI-for-testability (CLAUDE.md §5), not premature abstraction — the test fake is the second impl that earns the trait, so it does NOT violate REFACTOR-001's 'no abstraction over one call site' rule.

DO (inside record_extracted_learnings, per candidate):
- Keep Tier-1 `learning_exists(conn, &params.outcome, &params.title)?` exact-match check FIRST and UNCONDITIONAL (the only guard when guard is None / Ollama down).
- Then, if guard is Some, call check(&params.title, &params.content):
  - Duplicate { existing_id, similarity } -> increment deduped, eprintln a skip line including existing_id and the cosine (e.g. "Learning extraction: skipped near-duplicate of #<id> (cos=<score:.3>)"), continue.
  - Unique(emb) -> writer.record(conn, params); on Ok(result) push result.learning_id and call guard.register(result.learning_id, emb).
  - Unavailable -> fall through to the plain writer.record(...) path.
- Preserve LearningWriter construction-before / flush-after ordering (flush stays in extract_learnings_from_output, after the helper returns).
- Borrow note: check() is &self, register() is &mut self. Sequence so the &mut borrow happens after check() returns and after record() — don't hold check()'s result borrow across register().

DO NOT:
- Do NOT call store_embedding inline to skip the flush re-embed — accept the N+1 embed as a documented tradeoff (bypassing the writer violates the chokepoint).
- Do NOT change learning_exists or the ExtractionResult struct.
- Do NOT gate Tier-1 on Ollama state / guard presence — it always runs first.
- Do NOT apply this in learn.rs or import_learnings.

Edge cases:
- guard is None (tests / Ollama down): exact-match-only path, behavior unchanged.
- Two near-duplicate candidates in the SAME batch: second caught because the first was register()'d.
- First-ever learning (empty known set): records normally.
edgeCases: db_dir None: checker None, exact-match-only, unchanged behavior
Two same-batch near-dupes: second caught via register()
First-ever learning / empty known set: records normally
Per-candidate embed failure: Unavailable -> records the candidate

failureModes.cause: Ollama dies mid-loop after checker built
failureModes.expectedBehavior: per-candidate check() returns Unavailable; remaining candidates record via exact-match path

failureModes.cause: Threshold mis-calibrated (real distinct learning scores high)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: conservative 0.92 minimizes this; the id+cosine skip log makes any false positive diagnosable

userStories.notes: Insertion point: the `for params in params_list` recording loop in extract_learnings_from_output (src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs ~170-200); learning_exists is ~256-267. Move that loop into a private `record_extracted_learnings` helper taking Option<&mut dyn NearDupGuard> (review finding #1) so the Tier-2 arms are unit-testable with a fake — spawn_claude at ~mod.rs:105 makes the in-fn loop unreachable from tests otherwise. Records against a bare &Connection (NO transaction — flush is after the helper returns), so no tx/borrow conflict. LearningWriter lifecycle (construct before, record, flush after) per learning #2726 — keep it. Reuse NearDuplicateChecker/NearDupOutcome/NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD from FEAT-001; the near-miss calibration log lives in check() (FEAT-001), not here. Learning #1456: dedup key is (outcome,title) today — leave it; widening to title+content is out of scope. Learning #2135: sole caller is the unified iteration pipeline — verify wiring there.
qualityDimensions: Tier-1 exact match runs first and unconditionally; Tier-2 only augments when a guard is present
Test seam: record_extracted_learnings + NearDupGuard trait make all arms unit-testable without subprocess/Ollama (DI per CLAUDE.md §5)
Uncertainty records, never drops — Unavailable falls through to record
No chokepoint bypass — writer.record + flush ownership preserved
Skip path is observable (id + cosine logged) for threshold calibration
Intra-batch dedup correct via register() on every accepted Unique
userStories.title: Wire the embedding near-duplicate guard into learning auto-extraction

acceptanceCriteria: No duplicated cosine/compose/embedding-resolution logic (reuses existing fns)
NearDuplicateChecker methods each under ~30 lines, single responsibility
The extract_learnings_from_output loop body stays readable; extract a private helper only if it genuinely clarifies
Code follows existing embeddings/ingestion module patterns
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review the embedding-guard implementation for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and clarity.

DO: Check NearDuplicateChecker for single-responsibility, confirm cosine/compose/loader reuse (no duplicated similarity logic), confirm the ingestion loop stays readable (consider a small private helper if the per-candidate match arms grow past ~30 lines).
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before final review. Do NOT introduce a premature abstraction over the two call sites.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97). If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line note.
qualityDimensions: Pattern consistency with embeddings/ and ingestion/ modules
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: NearDuplicateChecker is reachable from production (extract_learnings_from_output), grep-verified
Positive: All errors handled without unwrap()/expect() in production paths (Ollama failures degrade to Unavailable/None)
Positive: FULL `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean (no scope filter)
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths added by this change
Negative: No dead-code / unused warnings for the new public items
Negative: Ollama-down behavior is byte-identical to pre-change (exact-match only) — confirmed by a db_dir-None test path
Documentation: src/learnings/CLAUDE.md 'Learning Creation Chokepoint' section notes the ingestion-path write-time embedding near-dup guard, NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD, and the intentional cross-model skip
Documentation: no literal model strings leaked (tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs passes)
Task update: remaining tasks reviewed; descriptions updated if APIs/assumptions changed
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or spawned as FIX-xxx with verifyCommand if >~12 unrelated)
If issues found: FIX-xxx / WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Verify the guard is reachable from the loop engine's iteration pipeline (extract_learnings_from_output is the only insertion point — grep callers). Verify no unwrap() in new paths. Verify graceful degradation (Ollama down) is preserved. Confirm src/learnings/CLAUDE.md is updated to document the new write-time near-dup guard in the 'Learning Creation Chokepoint' narrative. Run full cargo test / clippy / fmt (no scope filter).
DO NOT: Skip the wiring check. Do NOT scope the test suite — this is THE place the full suite runs.
userStories.notes: Do NOT stamp `model` here — runtime reviewModel config (currently claude-opus-4-8) owns the reviewer. Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` (no `model` on spawned FIX/WIRE-FIX — primaryRunner.byIdPrefix routes them). Watch for the stale slot-worktree test-binary gotcha (CLAUDE.md): mass fixture-read failures naming a removed -slot-N path are not regressions — `touch tests/<bin>.rs` and rebuild. If clean: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review'.
qualityDimensions: Guard wired to the loop-engine extraction path
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
